articles.title: Taiwan's opposition leader arrives in China for a 'Journey of Peace'
articles.summary: The visit takes place ahead of President Trump's own summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next month, where Taiwan is expected to be a top agenda item.

articles.title: In Georgia’s 14th District Special Runoff, Democrats Pulled Off Their Biggest Shift Yet
articles.summary: Shawn Harris lost by about 12 percentage points in the 14th Congressional District, but he shifted the district 25 points to the left.

articles.title: Hours Before News of Cease-Fire, Pope Leo XIV Issues Strong Rebuke of Trump
articles.summary: The first U.S.-born pontiff has consistently called for dialogue to resolve the war in the Middle East. He referred to Trump’s Iran threats as ‘truly unacceptable.’

articles.title: Telehealth abortion will remain available for now, after a federal judge's ruling
articles.summary: The abortion pill mifepristone must undergo a safety review by the FDA, the judge said. Louisiana's case seeking to ban its use through telemedicine will proceed after that review.

articles.title: Trump Backs Down, but Questions Remain Over Iran and the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Trump’s short-term intimidation may have worked, but the fundamental divides with Iran are as sharp as they were in February.

articles.title: Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization
articles.summary: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.

articles.title: With Threat to Wipe Out Iran’s Civilization, Trump’s Rhetoric Goes Beyond Bluster
articles.summary: The president’s violent rhetoric risks damaging his credibility as a negotiator and the country’s standing in the world.

articles.title: Only Trump Knows Why Bondi Was Fired as Attorney General, Blanche Says
articles.summary: In his first news conference since being elevated to acting attorney general, Todd Blanche said that “nobody has any idea” what led to Pam Bondi’s dismissal other than President Trump.

articles.title: ‘I Love Viktor’: Trump and Vance Cheer on Orban in Hungarian Race
articles.summary: The American leaders slathered on the praise for the nationalist standard-bearer just days before an election he could lose.

articles.title: To Boost Military Budget, Trump Targets Popular Programs at Home
articles.summary: Amid the war with Iran, the president has proposed to scale back some of the very programs meant to ease families’ financial burdens.

articles.title: The Artemis II crew saw parts of the moon never seen before. Here's what they said
articles.summary: The astronauts on Artemis II observed parts of the moon humans had never seen before. Their findings provide a scientific baseline — and sense of wonder — for future missions.

articles.title: A Moscow poet highlights the challenges of speaking out against the war in Ukraine
articles.summary: In Russia, protesting against the war in Ukraine can be dangerous. But one Russian poet living in Moscow, Vadim Dzyuba, is speaking out.

articles.title: Congressional Democrats raise alarm over Trump's comments on Iran
articles.summary: Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.

articles.title: ICE acknowledges it is using powerful spyware
articles.summary: In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.

articles.title: In a stark reversal, President Trump announces two-week ceasefire with Iran
articles.summary: President Trump has announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, contingent on their opening of the Strait of Hormuz. In an earlier online post, he had threatened "a whole civilization will die tonight."

articles.title: DOJ’s Civil Rights Division Investigates Cassidy Hutchinson, Who Testified Against Trump
articles.summary: It was a highly unusual move by Justice Department leadership to direct a case that appears to involve accusations of lying to Congress to a division that normally focuses on civil rights abuses.

articles.title: Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife
articles.summary: The Artemis II crew, led by Reid Wiseman, was the first to lay eyes on several craters on the far side of the moon. The astronauts want to name one of them after Carroll Wiseman, who died of cancer in

articles.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? Americans weigh options for saving gas
articles.summary: With gasoline prices averaging above $4 a gallon nationally, drivers are grappling with a sharp rise in fuel costs. Here are some ideas to consider if you're trying to cut your fuel costs.

articles.title: Australia charges ex‑soldier with 5 war‑crime murders in Afghanistan
articles.summary: Roberts-Smith is only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign to be charged with a war crime.

articles.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
articles.summary: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

articles.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire, suspending Trump's threat to annihilate Iran
articles.summary: After Iran ceded to President Trump's demand to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump backed down from his threat of wide-scale destruction of Iran's civilian and military infrastructure.

articles.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
articles.summary: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
